note: Despite the event name being CONTROL_ASSESSED, the handler is named on_control_failed and only handles the failure case. There is no separate handler for control_passed events.

engines_involved.method_2: add_control_gap(org_id, data) — requires assessment_id from method_1
engines_involved.role: primary — creates a compliance gap assessment then attaches a control gap record for the failed control
engines_involved.status: IMPLEMENTED (compliance_gap_engine.py confirmed on disk, classes and methods at correct line numbers)

trigger_sources: ComplianceAutomationEngine after running an automated control test
ControlTestingEngine when a control test result falls below threshold
Any engine calling event_bus.emit(CONTROL_ASSESSED, ...) with a failed status

registration.legacy_event_type: NOT REGISTERED — CONTROL_ASSESSED absent from legacy EventBus type_map

current_state.summary: Two-step sequential call to ComplianceGapEngine. Step 1 creates an assessment. Step 2 uses the returned assessment_id to add a control gap. The framework is validated against a 7-value allowlist. The two-step call is inside a single _safe_call wrapper meaning if create_assessment() succeeds but add_control_gap() fails, the exception is caught and the gap entry is silently lost.
current_state.gap_severity: Hardcoded 'medium' for all auto-detected gaps — no mapping from the control failure severity in the event payload.
current_state.isolation: Both steps are inside ONE _safe_call() — if step 2 (add_control_gap) raises, the exception is caught but the assessment created in step 1 is left as an orphaned empty assessment.

what_works: Two-step sequential pattern correctly uses the assessment_id returned by create_assessment() as input to add_control_gap().
Framework is normalised via allowlist with NIST fallback for unknown values.
Auto-assessment creation means compliance gap tracking starts immediately without requiring a human to create an assessment first.
control_id is forwarded as a structured field to add_control_gap().
what_doesnt: CONTROL_ASSESSED is missing from the legacy EventBus type_map.
handler is named on_control_failed but is registered for CONTROL_ASSESSED — all control assessments (pass AND fail) trigger it. Passed controls should not create gap entries.
Both create_assessment() and add_control_gap() are wrapped in a SINGLE _safe_call() — failure of add_control_gap() leaves an orphaned empty assessment that pollutes compliance dashboards.
Gap severity is hardcoded 'medium' — the actual control failure severity from the event payload is ignored.
A new assessment is created for every single failed control — there is no attempt to group multiple failed controls from the same scan/audit into a single assessment, leading to assessment explosion.
No deduplication — the same (org_id, entity_id) control failure creates a new assessment+gap every time the event fires.
No downstream chain — after creating the gap, no RISK_ASSESSED event fires and no posture recalculation is triggered.
CONTROL_ASSESSED with a passed result should trigger a gap closure/remediation rather than a new gap creation, but there is no passed-control handler.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given CONTROL_ASSESSED with status='failed', framework='SOC2', entity_id='CC6.1', ComplianceGapEngine.create_assessment() and add_control_gap() are both called with the correct org_id and control_id.
AC2: Given CONTROL_ASSESSED with status='passed', NO gap is created. The subscriber must inspect a 'status' field on the event and skip gap creation for passing controls.
AC3: Given CONTROL_ASSESSED with an unknown framework (e.g. 'NIST-CSF-2'), the framework falls back to 'NIST' and a WARNING log entry is emitted.
AC4: Gap severity must be derived from the event payload's 'severity' field (if present) rather than always defaulting to 'medium'.
AC5: create_assessment() and add_control_gap() must be called in two separate _safe_call() wrappers so that failure of add_control_gap() is isolated from assessment creation.
AC6: Multiple failed controls from the same scan_id (provided in event payload) must be grouped into a single assessment rather than creating one assessment per control.
AC7: A RISK_ASSESSED event must be emitted after gap creation so posture scoring is triggered.
AC8: CONTROL_ASSESSED must be added to the legacy EventBus type_map.
AC9: A pytest test covers the failed-control path (gap created), the passed-control path (no gap), and the orphan-assessment prevention (step-2 failure leaves no orphan).
